reasoning: GEOMETRY CHECK: (1) Column 0 vs column 31 — brick mortar lines appear to continue reasonably across the horizontal seam; no sharp discontinuity detected, horizontal tileability looks acceptable. (2) Row 0 vs row 31 — top row and bottom row are both neutral tan-brown mortar tones, vertical seam appears passable. (3) 3x3 mental tile test — this is where the tile fails: the colorful accent pixels (pink ~(8,10), red ~(12,6), orange ~(10,14), blue ~(14,8) etc.) are concentrated in the upper ~35% of the 32px height, while the lower 65% is near-uniform tan brick with minimal accent scatter. When tiled 3x3 this creates a conspicuous horizontal stripe of color noise repeating every 32px vertically, forming a visible polka-band pattern. The base brick mortar structure is coherent and the edge seams are workable, but the non-uniform vertical density of the accent/detail pixels is a tileability defect. As a background wall texture it should read as neutral at any zoom — the accent concentration breaks that. Score 5 marginal: edge seams ok, but vertical banding from accent clustering prevents clean pass.
